jokes.text: I thought the guest host was a big surprise. Did you know sandra was going to host the show? No. Surprise. She's hosted the show 11 times already. Well, surprise. She's going to do it again.
jokes.setup: Hank claims Sandra hosting is a 'big surprise'
jokes.punchline: She's hosted 11 times already, making it the opposite of surprising
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Hank's oblivious optimism vs Artie's dry reality check. Economy is excellent, structure builds nicely

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I thought the guest host was a big surprise. Did you know sandra was going to host the show?

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: No. Surprise.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: She's hosted the show 11 times already.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Well, surprise. She's going to do it again.

jokes.text: I swear to God, I forgot what the last thing was.
jokes.setup: Hank is given three simple things to remember about loving the job, guests, and Larry
jokes.punchline: He immediately forgets the third one despite just hearing it
jokes.explanation: Classic Hank incompetence - perfectly in character, good setup/payoff timing

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I swear to God, I forgot what the last thing was.

jokes.text: Yeah, Hank. Maybe you could show her your video where you're supporting one woman's rights while another woman is blowing you.
jokes.setup: Hank claims to be a supporter of women's rights
jokes.punchline: Artie references Hank's hypocritical sex tape
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character insight - exposes Hank's hypocrisy with specific, visual detail. Perfect Artie cynicism

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Yeah, Hank. Maybe you could show her your video where you're supporting one woman's rights while another woman is blowing you.

jokes.text: I also had my penis inverted into a vagina 'cause I figured once I had my eyes done, I'd want to get the whole thing done.
jokes.setup: Larry admits to getting his eyes done for cosmetic reasons
jokes.punchline: Escalates to claiming he had full gender reassignment surgery with casual logic
jokes.explanation: Unexpected escalation with perfect deadpan delivery. The casual logic ('figured I'd get the whole thing done') makes it funnier

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I also had my penis inverted into a vagina 'cause I figured once I had my eyes done, I'd want to get the whole thing done.

jokes.text: She's a nurse on ER.
jokes.setup: Larry mentions Julianna Margulies giving him medical advice about his surgery recovery
jokes.punchline: Artie points out she's just an actress playing a nurse
jokes.explanation: Perfect reality check delivered with Artie's characteristic brevity. Industry-insider humor

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: She's a nurse on ER.

jokes.text: Oh! And yet, I look at you, and I just want to be bald.
jokes.setup: Artie compliments Sandra as the sexiest woman in show business
jokes.punchline: She responds with wanting to be bald, which makes no logical sense
jokes.explanation: Classic Sandra Bernhard non-sequitur. The randomness feels authentically her but lacks strong comedic structure

dialogue.speaker: Sandra
dialogue.line: Oh! And yet, I look at you, and I just want to be bald.

jokes.text: Hank plays an indian who sits on his own arrow.
jokes.setup: Phil pitches a Dr. Quinn Medicine Woman parody
jokes.punchline: The visual of Hank's character sitting on his own arrow
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag that works on the absurd image. Economical and clear

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: Hank plays an indian who sits on his own arrow.

jokes.text: I mean, if they want me to be Larry, why don't they just dress me up in one of Larry's purple suits and have me limp over to the desk and act like I have no reason to live?
jokes.setup: Sandra complains about the show not wanting to do anything new
jokes.punchline: Brutally specific description of Larry's on-air persona as depressed and pathetic
jokes.explanation: Sharp character observation with specific visual details (purple suits, limping). Captures Larry's depressive on-air energy perfectly

dialogue.speaker: Sandra
dialogue.line: I mean, if they want me to be Larry, why don't they just dress me up in one of Larry's purple suits and have me limp over to the desk and act like I have no reason to live?

jokes.text: No. I just assumed it was near the freeway when it's called the on-ramp theatre.
jokes.setup: Phil asks if Brian knows where the On-Ramp Theatre is
jokes.punchline: Brian logically deduces it's near a freeway based purely on the name
jokes.explanation: Simple observational humor with good logical structure. Clean setup/punchline

dialogue.speaker: Brian
dialogue.line: No. I just assumed it was near the freeway when it's called the on-ramp theatre.

jokes.text: This guy's addicted to crack, and I have a scene where he can't decide whether to kill himself or to masturbate.
jokes.setup: Phil describes his 'serious' play about a TV writer
jokes.punchline: The supposedly deep artistic choice is between suicide and masturbation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phil character moment - his idea of profound art is crude and simplistic. Dark humor executed well

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: This guy's addicted to crack, and I have a scene where he can't decide whether to kill himself or to masturbate.

jokes.text: Please come, because if you don't come, I might start doing crack again.
jokes.setup: Phil desperately wants people to attend his play
jokes.punchline: He threatens to relapse into drug addiction if they don't come
jokes.explanation: Great character escalation - Phil's desperation and manipulation tactics perfectly captured

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: Please come, because if you don't come, I might start doing crack again.

jokes.text: We love our guests. I love my job.
jokes.setup: They were coached to give specific responses if Gloria Steinem asks questions
jokes.punchline: They robotically deliver the memorized lines out of order when meeting her
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier coaching scene. Shows how the staff can't handle even simple instructions

dialogue.line: We love our guests. I love my job.

jokes.text: Women, thank God, are now equal with men, and... although, I gotta tell you, I think men are just a little stronger... but in all other areas, equal. Especially at the crunch. You know... sex time.
jokes.setup: Hank tries to impress Gloria Steinem with his feminist views
jokes.punchline: He immediately undermines himself and ends with inappropriate sexual reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hank moment - his attempt at enlightenment immediately devolves into inappropriate sexual commentary. Brilliant cringe

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Women, thank God, are now equal with men, and... although, I gotta tell you, I think men are just a little stronger... but in all other areas, equal. Especially at the crunch. You know... sex time.

jokes.text: I was just busily pleasing myself, but now I realize, thanks to you, people like you, it's a 2-way street, and I want to thank you.
jokes.setup: Hank continues his inappropriate conversation with Gloria Steinem about sexual equality
jokes.punchline: He admits to previously only caring about his own pleasure in graphic terms while thanking her
jokes.explanation: Sustained cringe comedy at its finest. Hank's complete obliviousness to how inappropriate he's being

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I was just busily pleasing myself, but now I realize, thanks to you, people like you, it's a 2-way street, and I want to thank you.

jokes.text: Great ass.
jokes.setup: Gloria Steinem leaves after Hank's inappropriate conversation
jokes.punchline: Hank immediately objectifies her as she walks away
jokes.explanation: Perfect punctuation to the cringe sequence. Shows Hank learned nothing. Economy and timing are perfect

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Great ass.

jokes.text: Let me guess. Are you pissed because you didn't get to host again?
jokes.setup: Hank claims Sandra is trying to take over the show
jokes.punchline: Artie immediately identifies Hank's real motivation - jealousy
jokes.explanation: Perfect character insight - Artie sees through Hank's conspiracy theories to his petty jealousy

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Let me guess. Are you pissed because you didn't get to host again?

jokes.text: I'll have Paula give you the phone number of my buddy oliver stone.
jokes.setup: Hank insists there's a conspiracy to take over the show
jokes.punchline: Artie suggests he call Oliver Stone, famous for conspiracy theory movies
jokes.explanation: Clever pop culture reference that perfectly fits the situation. Good structure and economy

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: I'll have Paula give you the phone number of my buddy oliver stone.

jokes.text: I tell you there's a conspiracy, and you tell me to put on makeup, but then she walks in, tells you, and you're all ears. It's always about you, isn't it?
jokes.setup: Beverly also warns Artie about Sandra's manager
jokes.punchline: Hank points out the sexist double standard in how Artie treated their identical warnings
jokes.explanation: Surprisingly insightful moment for Hank - he correctly identifies the sexist dismissal. Good character development

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I tell you there's a conspiracy, and you tell me to put on makeup, but then she walks in, tells you, and you're all ears. It's always about you, isn't it?

jokes.text: Who knew I was gonna come floating out here in a pair of manolo blahnik lucite high heels... that I happened to borrow from Larry.
jokes.setup: Sandra makes her entrance as guest host
jokes.punchline: She claims to be wearing Larry's designer high heels
jokes.explanation: Good visual absurdity - the image of Larry owning designer women's shoes. Fits Sandra's style

dialogue.speaker: Sandra
dialogue.line: Who knew I was gonna come floating out here in a pair of manolo blahnik lucite high heels... that I happened to borrow from Larry.

jokes.text: but I'm just here to kind of abuse you again and tell you that's what I do. Nyah! No.
jokes.setup: Sandra talks about rich people discarding clothes while poor people shop at discount stores
jokes.punchline: She admits she's just there to abuse the audience and makes a childish face
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on her own hosting style. Self-aware and authentically Sandra

dialogue.speaker: Sandra
dialogue.line: but I'm just here to kind of abuse you again and tell you that's what I do. Nyah! No.

jokes.text: The managers usually stay in the green room by the shrimp plate.
jokes.setup: Someone (likely Ellen) tries to interrupt Sandra's monologue
jokes.punchline: Sandra dismisses them by noting where managers typically belong
jokes.explanation: Good put-down with specific detail about show business hierarchy. Economical and sharp

dialogue.speaker: Sandra
dialogue.line: The managers usually stay in the green room by the shrimp plate.

jokes.text: All right? Now, I'm not saying that I want to see Gary on the cover of allure, but I can bet one thing. Naomi campbell wouldn't have dragged her tired ass over to my house to fix my hot water heater, OK?
jokes.setup: Sandra talks about how we worship supermodels instead of useful workers
jokes.punchline: Contrasts Gary the plumber's reliability with Naomi Campbell's likely unwillingness to do plumbing
jokes.explanation: Good observational premise with specific examples. The 'tired ass' description adds personality

dialogue.speaker: Sandra
dialogue.line: All right? Now, I'm not saying that I want to see Gary on the cover of allure, but I can bet one thing. Naomi campbell wouldn't have dragged her tired ass over to my house to fix my hot water heater, OK?

jokes.text: I know you got this thing set up to hide the bolts in Larry's neck.
jokes.setup: Sandra comments on the lighting setup
jokes.punchline: She suggests it's designed to hide Larry's Frankenstein-like neck bolts
jokes.explanation: Creative insult with specific visual imagery. Implies Larry is artificially constructed like Frankenstein's monster

dialogue.speaker: Sandra
dialogue.line: I know you got this thing set up to hide the bolts in Larry's neck.

jokes.text: That's a rabbit.
jokes.setup: Sandra asks why the snake is big through the middle
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact explanation that there's a whole rabbit inside the snake
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of disturbing information. The casual nature makes it funnier

dialogue.speaker: Rob
dialogue.line: That's a rabbit.

jokes.text: That's the rectum.
jokes.setup: Sandra asks about a hole on the snake
jokes.punchline: Clinical identification of the snake's anus on live TV
jokes.explanation: Simple cringe humor from inappropriate TV content. The expert's clinical tone adds to the awkwardness

dialogue.speaker: Rob
dialogue.line: That's the rectum.

jokes.text: Hank, want to get in here and do a quick exam? No. Just kidding.
jokes.setup: They've just identified the snake's rectum
jokes.punchline: Sandra jokingly invites Hank to examine it; he refuses immediately
jokes.explanation: Good setup/punchline structure. Hank's immediate 'No' is perfectly timed

dialogue.speaker: Sandra
dialogue.line: Hank, want to get in here and do a quick exam?

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: No.

dialogue.speaker: Sandra
dialogue.line: Just kidding.

jokes.text: Well, that depends on what day of the week it is. I believe on Monday it's Manhattan clam chowder.
jokes.setup: Someone asks what's up Hank's ass
jokes.punchline: Artie provides a specific, food-based schedule for what's bothering Hank
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist response to common expression. The specificity of 'Manhattan clam chowder' makes it exceptional

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Well, that depends on what day of the week it is. I believe on Monday it's Manhattan clam chowder.

jokes.text: I think we should give Hank the next 2 weeks off. What do you think?
jokes.setup: Artie is having back problems and Ellen is supposedly being helpful
jokes.punchline: She suggests giving Hank time off instead of the person with the actual injury
jokes.explanation: Reveals Ellen's manipulative nature perfectly - using Artie's injury to get rid of Hank

dialogue.speaker: Ellen
dialogue.line: I think we should give Hank the next 2 weeks off. What do you think?

jokes.text: You know what, Larry? I can't check the swelling if you don't let me touch it.
jokes.setup: Larry asks Julianna not to touch him while he's on the phone
jokes.punchline: She needs to physically examine his post-surgery swelling
jokes.explanation: Mild cringe humor from the awkward medical examination situation

dialogue.speaker: Julianna
dialogue.line: You know what, Larry? I can't check the swelling if you don't let me touch it.

jokes.text: You start tightening up your face, it just makes your ass look enormous.
jokes.setup: Discussion about why she wouldn't have plastic surgery
jokes.punchline: The supposed side effect of facial surgery making your ass look bigger
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor with unexpected logic. The specific body part reference adds punch

dialogue.speaker: Julianna
dialogue.line: You start tightening up your face, it just makes your ass look enormous.

jokes.text: I think it's learned how to read.
jokes.setup: Artie's back pain flares up when shown the negative newspaper article
jokes.punchline: He suggests his back has become literate and is reacting to bad news
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor with perfect economy. The idea of a literate back is genuinely original and funny

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: I think it's learned how to read.

jokes.text: Do you know if he's gay? Because I'd blow him.
jokes.setup: Phil desperately wants a good review from the L.A. Weekly critic
jokes.punchline: He casually offers oral sex as payment for a positive review
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phil character moment - his artistic desperation knows no bounds. The casual delivery sells it

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: Do you know if he's gay? Because I'd blow him.

jokes.text: You will? You'll get down on your knees and you'll put his penis in your mouth?
jokes.setup: Phil casually offers to perform oral sex for a good review
jokes.punchline: Brian makes him confront the graphic reality of what he's suggesting
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that forces Phil to confront his desperation. The explicit description creates the comedy

dialogue.speaker: Brian
dialogue.line: You will? You'll get down on your knees and you'll put his penis in your mouth?

jokes.text: Well, since you put it that way, I guess it's not so appealing.
jokes.setup: Brian explicitly describes what Phil is offering to do
jokes.punchline: Phil immediately backtracks when faced with the graphic reality
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the setup. Shows Phil's bravado evaporates when confronted with reality

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: Well, since you put it that way, I guess it's not so appealing.

jokes.text: She's evil and fat and she wants your show.
jokes.setup: Beverly explains why Ellen is a threat to Larry
jokes.punchline: She includes 'fat' as one of Ellen's threatening characteristics alongside evil ambition
jokes.explanation: Character comedy showing Beverly's cattiness. The physical insult alongside the professional threat is telling

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: She's evil and fat and she wants your show.

jokes.text: Maybe Mark fuhrman planted it.
jokes.setup: Larry questions who planted the negative article about him
jokes.punchline: Reference to the controversial O.J. Simpson trial detective as a conspiracy scapegoat
jokes.explanation: Good topical reference that was very current. Shows Larry deflecting with absurd conspiracy theories

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Maybe Mark fuhrman planted it.

jokes.text: Friday is 'mystery meat.'
jokes.setup: Network plans different guest hosts throughout the week
jokes.punchline: Friday's host is unknown, compared to cafeteria mystery meat
jokes.explanation: Clever metaphor comparing unknown guest host to mystery meat. Good economy and structure

dialogue.line: Friday is 'mystery meat.'

jokes.text: I've had no surgery at all.
jokes.setup: Executive suggests Larry should take another week off after surgery
jokes.punchline: Larry completely denies having any surgery despite obvious evidence
jokes.explanation: Good callback to his earlier surgery admission. Shows Larry's vanity and denial

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I've had no surgery at all.

jokes.text: In the middle of the second act, a car flew off the freeway, crashed through the wall of the theater, and took out the first couple of rows. Fortunately, no one was there.
jokes.setup: Phil's play at the On-Ramp Theatre closed on Friday
jokes.punchline: Elaborate explanation involving a car crash that reveals no one attended anyway
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback to the freeway location joke. The elaborate disaster story ending with 'no one was there' is perfectly crafted

dialogue.line: In the middle of the second act, a car flew off the freeway, crashed through the wall of the theater, and took out the first couple of rows. Fortunately, no one was there.

jokes.text: Oh, he's on crack.
jokes.setup: Phil seems to be handling his play's failure well
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact explanation that he's using drugs
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to Phil's crack addiction reference. Beverly's casual delivery makes it funnier

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: Oh, he's on crack.

jokes.text: Um... 3-8. But sandra got a younger demographic.
jokes.setup: Network executive reveals Sandra got the same ratings as Larry
jokes.punchline: Tries to spin identical numbers as somehow better for Sandra
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of network executive spin. The pause before adding the demographic qualifier is expertly timed

dialogue.speaker: Network Executive
dialogue.line: Um... 3-8. But sandra got a younger demographic.

jokes.text: I was just saying to my dog Bob packwood, I said...
jokes.setup: Larry sets up a joke about sexual harassment in animals
jokes.punchline: Names his dog after the disgraced senator known for sexual harassment
jokes.explanation: Good topical reference that was very current. Shows Larry's monologue style of connecting current events

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I was just saying to my dog Bob packwood, I said...

jokes.text: evidently hoping to take another whack at it.
jokes.setup: John Wayne Bobbitt showed up at his wife's workplace with flowers
jokes.punchline: Double meaning of 'whack' referring both to attempting reconciliation and the infamous incident
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on the Bobbitt case. The double meaning is well-crafted and economical

dialogue.line: evidently hoping to take another whack at it.

jokes.text: Maybe 8 years of Larry is enough.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Larry's ratings and charm
jokes.punchline: Executive casually suggests Larry might have run his course
jokes.explanation: Perfect network executive moment - the casual brutality of show business expressed in understated terms

dialogue.speaker: Network Executive
dialogue.line: Maybe 8 years of Larry is enough.
